mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 50%) or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values during a monetary tightening cycle reflect resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during tightening periods are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC <= 0.01) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL reading above 240.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly declines in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirm a synchronized demand contraction, forcing earnings downgrades and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual decline signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns (win rate < 45%) or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid supply chain normalization and inventory restocking, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases in recovery regimes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, or the regime-specific predictive power is not statistically significant.
